T1.title: Inventory backend implementations and conformance coverage
T1.evidence: Inventory in memory backend-inventory-T1. Suite runs file+git only; postgres/redis registered behind build tags but untested. Gaps: R2.3 monotone, R3.2/R3.3, R4.2 atomicity, R4.3 git-commit-per-write. Investigator, verify N/A.

T2.title: Parameterize conformance over all four backends with graceful skips
T2.evidence: backend table file/git/postgres/redis; postgres/redis skip with explicit reason; file+git run. go test -run Conformance -v → exit 0 (PASS + 2 SKIP). go vet -tags 'specd_postgres specd_redis' clean.

T3.title: CAS + revision monotonicity tests across backends
T3.evidence: CAS+monotone (rev 1→6), stale-base rejected no clobber, 32-goroutine Turn==32. go test -run 'Conformance|Concurren' -race → exit 0.

T4.title: Lock recovery + durability/atomicity tests
T4.evidence: lock-released-on-completion (R3.3), backend-agnostic schema-valid-after-write (R4.1/4.2), git one-commit-per-revision (R4.3). go test -run 'Lock|Conformance|Replay' → exit 0.

T5.title: Review backend parity and document service requirements
T5.evidence: Reviewed T2-T4: skips are visible t.Skip w/ reasons, no silent passes. Added TESTING.md backend-parity section (skip table + tag/env setup + no-driver guarantee). Caveat recorded in memory parity-caveat-T5. go test -run Conformance -v → exit 0.
